name: Fish Hunan Style
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lbs. fish filets (red snapper, cod, pollock, etc.)
3 tbsp. dry sherry or apple cider vinegar
1 tbsp. crushed red pepper (or to taste!)
1 tbsp. cornstarch, mixed with 1 tbsp. water
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the marinade by combining soy sauce, sherry (or vinegar), ginger, garlic, and green onion.
2. Cut the fish into about 2-inch pieces and marinate for 30-40 minutes.
3. Remove the fish from the marinade and set aside. Save the marinade for later use as a sauce.
4. Dip each piece of fish in beaten egg, then coat with floured bread crumbs.
5. Fry the fish in about an inch of shortening until nicely browned. Set aside.
6. To make the sauce, add red pepper, chicken broth, and brown sugar to the reserved marinade. Bring to a boil.
7. Add the cornstarch-water mixture to the sauce and bring to a boil once more.
8. Serve the sauce hot over fish with noodles or rice.
